personal.name: Kishan Rajdev
personal.title: Full-Stack Engineer | Researcher
personal.bio: I am a master's student in Computer Science (thesis) at Troy University with a strong focus on research. During my graduate studies, I have worked on multiple research projects spanning machine learning, computer vision, uncertainty estimation, trustworthy AI, and interdisciplinary data analysis in sports analytics. I have a solid foundation in computer science fundamentals and currently maintain a 4.0 GPA in my graduate studies. I also have prior industry experience as a full-stack engineer in startups and product-based companies, where I contributed to developing scalable real-world systems. I am interested in pursuing a research-oriented career in academia or industry.

areas.title: The Computational Efficiency-Performance Trade-Off in Non-Bayesian Ensemble Methods for Image Classification
areas.description: Evaluated ensemble methods for uncertainty estimation in deep learning and analyzed how model diversity influences robustness under distribution shift, comparing Deep Ensembles, Snapshot Ensembles, Fast Geometric Ensembling, and Noisy Deep Ensembles on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-10-C, and iSUN benchmarks. Under review at IMPROVE 2026.

areas.title: Diversity-Enhanced Snapshot Ensembles (DESE)
areas.description: Proposed a lightweight extension of Snapshot Ensembles that improves robustness by selecting diverse snapshots using cosine similarity in parameter space and prediction disagreement in output space on a validation set. Accepted at ACMSE 2026.

areas.title: Uncertainty-Aware Skin Cancer Classification with Deep Ensembles
areas.description: Evaluated Deep Ensembles for seven-class skin lesion classification using the ISIC dataset, demonstrating improved diagnostic accuracy and well-calibrated uncertainty estimates for risk-sensitive medical applications. Accepted as a poster at ACMSE 2026.

areas.title: Show Me the Money: Factors Contributing to Collegiate Football NIL Valuations
areas.description: Contributed to an interdisciplinary research project with Troy University's Sports Management faculty, performing data preprocessing and ANCOVA to analyze factors influencing collegiate football NIL valuations. Accepted in Sport Marketing Quarterly.

areas.title: Secure Standard-Based Approach to Cloud Interoperability in E-Government
areas.description: Contributed to research on improving cloud interoperability in e-government systems using a multi-cloud architecture to mitigate vendor lock-in by conducting interoperability experiments across Azure and AWS. Accepted at CloudTech'25.
